sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
முத்தமிழ் பாவினால் இசை பாட முனைந்தேன்
உத்தமனே உமையாள் மைந்தனே வருக
sections.translit:
mutthamizh pAvinAl isai pAda munaindhEn
utthamanE umaiyAL maindhanE varuga
sections.en: I began to sing your glory in pure Tamil language that has prose, poetry and drama as its three components. Oh, virtuous one and Uma’s son, please come.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
குன்றினின்று விழுந்தானை குமரன் அடியானை
மன்றினில் மாண்புடன் இலங்கிட நாவில் நீ எழுதிய
sections.translit:
kundRinindRu vizhundhAnai kumaran adiyAnai
mandRinil mANbudan ilangida nAvil nI ezhudhiya
sections.en: I use the same medium, this Tamil language, which you used to write with your spear on the devotee’s tongue and made him a prominent poet in an assembly of intellectuals. You came at a time when the devotee was going to fall off a cliff.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
மயிலேறி வந்தே மாயமாய் தடுத்தாய்
அவலநிலை நீக்கி அன்பனை அணைத்தாய்
முத்தைத்திருவென முதலடியும் ஈந்தாய்
வித்தை தருவோனே எத்தையும் நீயேயென்றெண்ணி
sections.translit:
mayilERi vandhE mAyamAy thadutthAy
avalanilai nIkki anbanai aNaitthAy
mutthaitthiruvena mudhaladiyum  IndhAy
vitthai tharuvOnE etthaiyum nIyEyendReNNi
sections.en: You came riding on a peacock and like magic, you stopped him at the right time. You embraced this devotee and removed the pathetic condition he was in. You even inscribed the first line to begin with the word “Mutthaith thiru” and made him a renowned poet. Oh, Lord, who grants learning, I realized that you are the ultimate for everything in life, and I sing your glory. Author’s Note Reference is made in the Anupallavi and Charanam to Saint Arunagirinathar’s story. Lord Muruga saved Arunagirinathar who had lost everything in life: wealth, relatives, and reputation due to his evil ways. For details on how the Lord inscribed on Arunagirinathar’s tongue with his spear and allowed to him to compose Thiruppugazh spontaneously, see: Life story of Arunagirinathar
